question.content: Compute the value of the given line integral along curve C, where C is the intersection of the cylinder and plane and the direction is clockwise when viewed from +z. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Use the variable names specified in the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Build the spatial geometry of the cylinder and the cutting plane.
step_1.key_point: The target curve is their intersection.

step_3.content: Parameterize the curve by x=cos(t), y=sin(t), z=2-cos(t)+sin(t).
step_3.key_point: Clockwise orientation corresponds to decreasing t.

step_5.content: Substitute into the differential form and simplify to the integrand 1-4*cos(t)^2.
step_5.key_point: Integrate on t in [0,2*pi] with the orientation sign.
